name: Intel Pentium SX835
description:
Key Specifications:

Clock Speed: 60 MHz with a 60 MHz front-side bus. 
Socket: Socket 4 (specifically designed for 5V processors). 
Package: Ceramic PGA (Pin Grid Array) with a distinctive gold-plated heat spreader ("Goldcap"). 
Cache: 16 KB L1 cache (8 KB for instructions, 8 KB for data). 
Process: 0.8 micron manufacturing technology. 
Notable Feature: The C1 stepping (SX835) is famously associated with the FDIV bug, a hardware error in the floating-point unit that caused incorrect division results in rare cases.  This defect led to a massive recall by Intel.
